text.title: choreography-vs-orchestration-decision Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the choreography-vs-orchestration-decision methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Author writes pattern='hybrid' with no semantics defined.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wants to defer the call.
antipattern.fix: Force a binary choice; if the workflow truly has two phases, split into two ADRs (one per phase).

antipattern.root-cause: Pressure to avoid naming a single person.

antipattern.symptom: Decision lists only the chosen option without comparing trade-offs.
antipattern.root-cause: Rationale not built from typed inputs.
antipattern.fix: Add a trade-off table referencing operational SLOs, latency budget, ownership map.

antipattern.symptom: One ADR claims to govern checkout + refund + cancellation simultaneously.
antipattern.fix: Split into one ADR per workflow.
